title: pouteria erringtonii
intro: Pouteria erringtonii, also known as the Burdekin plum or bush plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Australia. The plant's bark, leaves, and fruit have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The Burdekin plum is also valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and in some cases, the fruit is eaten fresh or used to make jams and preserves.
